name: Complement
definition_en: The complement system is a cascade of ~30 blood proteins that punch holes in pathogens, tag them for eating, and amplify inflammation.

name: Innate Immunity
definition_en: Innate immunity is the body's inherited, first-line defense — fast, broad, and the same in every person.

name: Macrophage
definition_en: A macrophage is a long-lived innate immune cell that eats pathogens, cleans debris, and signals the rest of the immune system.

name: Neutrophil
definition_en: A neutrophil is the most abundant white blood cell — a short-lived innate phagocyte that arrives first at infection sites.

name: NK Cell
definition_en: An NK (natural killer) cell is an innate lymphocyte that kills virally infected and cancerous cells without needing prior exposure.

name: Adaptive Immunity
definition_en: Adaptive immunity is the body's tailored defense — slow at first, but precise, and it remembers.

name: B Cell
definition_en: A B cell is the antibody-producing arm of adaptive immunity — it becomes either a plasma cell or a memory cell.

name: Cytotoxic T Cell
definition_en: A cytotoxic T cell (CD8+) is the targeted killer of the adaptive system — it destroys infected or cancerous cells directly.

name: Helper T Cell
definition_en: A helper T cell (CD4+) is the orchestrator of the adaptive immune response — it activates B cells and macrophages.

name: Memory Cell
definition_en: A memory cell is a long-lived T or B cell that 'remembers' a past antigen and responds explosively on re-exposure.

name: Dendritic Cell
definition_en: A dendritic cell is a sentinel that captures antigens in tissues and migrates to lymph nodes to teach T cells.

name: Lymph Node
definition_en: A lymph node is a bean-shaped filter where dendritic cells meet T cells — where adaptive immunity is born.

name: Mast Cell
definition_en: A mast cell is a tissue-resident innate cell that releases histamine — the central player in allergy.

name: Plasma Cell
definition_en: A plasma cell is a terminally differentiated B cell — an antibody factory.

name: Regulatory T Cell
definition_en: A regulatory T cell (Treg) is a subtype that brakes the immune response — preventing autoimmunity.

name: Antibody
definition_en: An antibody (immunoglobulin) is a Y-shaped protein made by B cells that recognises one specific antigen.

name: Cytokine
definition_en: A cytokine is a small signalling protein that immune cells use to talk to each other.

name: IgE
definition_en: IgE is the antibody class that drives allergic reactions by arming mast cells.

name: Interferon
definition_en: An interferon is a cytokine that 'interferes' with viral replication and activates immune cells.

name: MHC
definition_en: MHC (Major Histocompatibility Complex) is a set of cell-surface molecules that display peptide fragments to T cells.

name: Adjuvant
definition_en: An adjuvant is a substance added to a vaccine to boost the immune response to the antigen.

name: Attenuated Vaccine
definition_en: An attenuated vaccine uses a weakened but live form of a pathogen to induce a strong, long-lasting response.

name: mRNA Vaccine
definition_en: An mRNA vaccine delivers messenger RNA encoding a viral protein, which cells then make and display.

name: Vaccine Schedule
definition_en: A vaccine schedule is the recommended timeline of vaccinations from infancy through adulthood.

name: Vaccine
definition_en: A vaccine is a preparation that trains adaptive immunity to recognise a pathogen — without causing the disease.

name: Allergy
definition_en: An allergy is an immune reaction to a normally harmless substance, mediated mostly by IgE and mast cells.

name: Anaphylaxis
definition_en: Anaphylaxis is a severe, systemic allergic reaction that can become fatal within minutes.

name: Atopy
definition_en: Atopy is the genetic tendency to develop IgE-mediated allergies — eczema, asthma, hay fever, food allergy.

name: Histamine
definition_en: Histamine is a small molecule released by mast cells that causes itch, swelling, and bronchoconstriction.

name: Hygiene Hypothesis
definition_en: The hygiene hypothesis proposes that reduced early-life microbial exposure increases allergy and autoimmune risk.

name: Autoimmunity
definition_en: Autoimmunity is a state where the immune system attacks the body's own tissues, mistaking 'self' for 'non-self'.

name: Immunodeficiency
definition_en: Immunodeficiency is a state of weakened or absent immune function — increasing infection susceptibility.

name: Multiple Sclerosis
definition_en: Multiple sclerosis (MS) is an autoimmune disease where T cells attack the myelin sheath of nerve cells in the CNS.

name: Tolerance
definition_en: Immune tolerance is the failure-mode-avoiding property: the system learns NOT to attack self.

name: Type 1 Diabetes
definition_en: Type 1 diabetes is an autoimmune disease where T cells destroy the insulin-producing beta cells of the pancreas.

name: Exercise and Immunity
definition_en: Exercise has a J-shaped dose-response with immunity — moderate boosts it, extreme exhausts it.

name: Gut Microbiome
definition_en: The gut microbiome is the trillions of bacteria, archaea, fungi, and viruses living in the intestines — central to immune calibration.

name: Sleep and Immunity
definition_en: Sleep, especially deep slow-wave sleep, consolidates immune memory and recalibrates inflammation.

name: Stress and Immunity
definition_en: Stress modulates immunity through the HPA axis — cortisol suppresses lymphocytes while shifting cytokine balance.

name: Sunlight Vitamin D
definition_en: Sunlight on skin synthesises vitamin D, which directly regulates immune cell function — modulating both innate and adaptive arms.

name: Allergen
definition_en: An allergen is any substance — food, pollen, venom, or drug — that triggers an IgE-mediated immune reaction in a sensitised individual.

name: Sensitization
definition_en: Sensitisation is the silent first phase of allergy — repeated allergen exposure trains B cells to produce IgE, arming mast cells for future reactions.

name: Eczema
definition_en: Eczema (atopic dermatitis) is a chronic inflammatory skin condition driven by a disrupted barrier and Th2-skewed immunity, often the first step of the atopic march.

name: Asthma
definition_en: Asthma is a chronic airway condition of reversible bronchoconstriction, typically triggered by allergens, exercise, or infection in atopic individuals.

name: Allergic Rhinitis
definition_en: Allergic rhinitis (hay fever) is IgE-mediated inflammation of the nasal mucosa causing sneezing, itching, and congestion in response to airborne allergens.

name: Food Allergy
definition_en: A food allergy is an IgE-mediated immune reaction to a specific food protein, ranging from hives and vomiting to life-threatening anaphylaxis.

name: Urticaria
definition_en: Urticaria (hives) is a skin reaction of raised, itchy wheals caused by mast cell histamine release, triggered by allergens, infections, or stress.

name: Cross-Reactivity
definition_en: Cross-reactivity occurs when antibodies trained against one allergen also bind a structurally similar protein in a different substance, causing unexpected reactions.

name: Epinephrine
definition_en: Epinephrine (adrenaline) is the first-line emergency treatment for anaphylaxis, rapidly reversing vasodilation and bronchoconstriction within minutes.

name: Allergen Immunotherapy
definition_en: Allergen immunotherapy gradually desensitises the immune system through controlled, escalating allergen exposure — the only disease-modifying treatment for allergy.
